title: auth — cache keys + tags
description: Auth caches back the hot login/verify paths. MFA challenge state lives in Redis (not Postgres) so a spike in challenge throughput doesn't hit the DB. JWKS is cached to serve /.well-known/jwks.json without a DB round-trip. Rate-limit buckets are Redis-native. Session-scope lookups (PAT → application_id + tenant_id) are cached under short TTL so PAT revocation propagates within 60 seconds.

keys.description: The JSON blob served by /.well-known/jwks.json. Rebuilt on JwtSigningKeyRotated event OR when the underlying auth_jwt_signing_keys table changes. TTL matches auth.jwt.jwks_cache_control max-age (default 3600s).
The currently-active signing key row (redacted — the decrypted secret is a MEMORY-ONLY cache, see below).

The DECRYPTED HS256 shared secret. MEMORY-ONLY (process-scoped) — NEVER persisted to Redis. Held for 60s + drops on process exit. Fresh KMS-decrypt per key on cache miss. Listed here for documentation only; Redis is NOT the backing store.
keys.notes: IMPORTANT: this cache does NOT go through Redis. It's a process-scoped in-memory cache. Persisting the plaintext to Redis would defeat KMS envelope-encryption's blast-radius guarantee.

keys.description: In-flight MFA challenge state (context blob + attempts_count). Auth's version of identity/caches.json's idn:mfa_challenge:{challenge_id} — identity ships the fallback pattern; auth OWNS the actual key.
Per-IP login attempt counter for auth.rate_limit_login middleware. 15-minute sliding window.
Per-email login attempt counter. 1-hour sliding window.
Per-email password-reset request counter. 1-hour sliding window.
Deny-list membership check. TTL matches the underlying JWT's remaining lifetime. The full deny-list table is read at process start + refreshed via pubsub on JwtRevoked events.
Resolved (user_id, application_id, tenant_id, roles, permissions) for a Sanctum PAT. Populated on first request; invalidated on SessionRevoked / user.state_change.
keys.notes: Short TTL is deliberate — session revocation must propagate within 60 seconds regardless of the invalidation event.

keys.description: Recent device fingerprints per User (last 30 days). Used by auth.detect_new_device middleware to distinguish new vs seen devices without a Postgres lookup.
Grant metadata (source_app, target_app, identity_id, expires_at) for the exchange path's fast-lookup. Fallback to Postgres on cache miss.

never_cached: JwtSigningKey.secret_encrypted (ciphertext IS cached in Redis under auth:signing_key:active; the DECRYPTED plaintext is memory-only — see the note under auth:signing_key:secret:{kid} above)
